summary:
For optimal results without causing tissue damage or compromising seal integrity, place MTA within a range extending no more than 3 millimeters beneath the apex during procedures involving root-end fillings. This ensures adequate coverage against leakage yet remains safe from potential inflammatory responses around periodontal structures. Agent2 concluded that based on general clinical practice, an appropriate placement extends roughly three millimeters under the tooth’s tip when performing such treatments. 

The summary captures essential details related directly addressing how deep one ought position this specific type of cement used medically inside teeth roots ends ensuring proper function alongside safety considerations concerning surrounding soft biological materials involved therein.
